examples.text: فَجَعَلْنَاهَا حَصِيدًا كَأَنْ لَمْ تَغْنَ بِالْأَمْسِ كَذٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
examples.english: We make it as a harvest, as if it had not flourished yesterday. Thus do We explain in detail the signs for a people who give thought.

body.text: A verb of the class called incomplete, (
) because, with the agent which it comprises, or to which it relates, it cannot constitute a complete proposition; i. e., non-attributive. The other verbs of this class are,

body.text_bw: maA daAma maAA@nofak~a maA fatiy^a maAbariHa maA zaAla baAta
body.text_ar: مَا دَامَ مَاا@نْفَكَّ مَا فَتِيَ مَابَرِحَ مَا زَالَ بَاتَ

body.text: . Each of these governs its noun, or subject, in the nom. case, and its enunciative, or predicate, in the acc. case; as,
and the like is often irregularly elided.) ―         -b3-
, divested of all signification of time. is often used as a copula. (See De Sacy 's Gr. Ar. i. 196.) So too is
as a complete, i. e., an attributive, verb, see

entries.body_html:
كأن: كَأَنَ: اشتَدَّ. وكَأَنْتُ: اشْتَدَدْت وكأَنَّ، بالتشديد: ذكرت

في ترجمة أَنن.

entries.lexicon_en: Arabic-English Lexicon by Edward William Lane (d. 1876)

entries.body_html:
ك

أن1 كَأَنَ فِى خَلْقِهِ He, (a man) was strong in his make (IAar, in TA, voce كَنَتَ.) كَأْنِىٌّ A man strong in his make. (IAar, in TA, voce كَنَتَ.)
